LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARILU ANGELES, METZI HERNANDEZ AND EMELIN HERNANDEZ'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANTS DISCHARGED THEIR DUTY TO PLAINTIFF UNDER RUTTENBERG V. RUTTENBERG (1997) 53 CAL.APP.4TH 801 BY NAMING AND SERVING PLAINTIFF IN THE WRONGFUL DEATH ACTION AS DISCLOSED BY THE COMPLAINT. SINCE ALL OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS TURN ON THIS ALLEGED DUTY, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT. IN DETERMINING WHETHER PLAINTIFF COULD AMEND, THE COURT HAS TAKEN JUDICIAL NOTICE OF CERTAIN PLEADINGS IN THE UNDERLYING CASE - D, E, F AND N. THOSE RECORDS CONFIRM PLAINTIFF'S SERVICE OF PROCESS, PLAINTIFF'S FILING OF A CROSS-COMPLAINT AND PLAINTIFF'S RECOVERY OF A PORTION OF THE SETTLEMENT AFTER A CONTESTED HEARING. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-02-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MARILU ANGELES, METZI HERNANDEZ AND EMELIN HERNANDEZ'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND FOR THE REASONS STATED IN THE COURT MINUTES OF AUGUST 2, 2007. BRIEFLY, DEFENDANTS DISCHARGED THEIR DUTY TO PLAINTIFF UNDER RUTTENBERG V RUTTENBERG (1997) 53 CAL APP 4TH 801. THIS IS DEMONSTRATED BY PLAINTIFF'S ACTIVE PARTICIPATION IN THE UNDERLYING LITIGATION. THE DELAY IN SERVING PLAINTIFF DID NOT IMPEDE HER ABILITY TO ASSERT HER RIGHTS AS TO THE ECONOMIC BENEFITS FOR THE HARM CAUSED TO HER BY THE LOSS OF HER FATHER. DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MARILU ANGELES, METZI HERNANDEZ AND EMELIN HERNANDEZ'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: JOSHUA S. MARKOWITZ, ESQUIRE (CARCIONE CATTERMOLE, ET AL., 650.367.6811) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JOHN M. ANTON, ESQUIRE (BOXER GERSON, 510.835.8870) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT MARILU ANGELES, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM. ANTONIO RUIZ, ESQUIRE AND CONCHITA LOZANO-BATISTA, ESQUIRE (510.337.1001) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND FOR THE REASONS STATED IN THE COURT MINUTES OF AUGUST 2, 2007. BRIEFLY, DEFENDANTS DISCHARGED THEIR DUTY TO PLAINTIFF UNDER RUTTENBERG V RUTTENBERG (1997) 53 CAL APP 4TH 801. THIS IS DEMONSTRATED BY PLAINTIFF'S ACTIVE PARTICIPATION IN THE UNDERLYING LITIGATION. THE DELAY IN SERVING PLAINTIFF DID NOT IMPEDE HER ABILITY TO ASSERT HER RIGHTS AS TO THE ECONOMIC BENEFITS FOR THE HARM CAUSED TO HER BY THE LOSS OF HER FATHER. DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Off Calendar Aug-23-2007 Continued To Oct-12-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MARILU ANGELES, METZI HERNANDEZ AND EMELIN HERNANDEZ'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: JOSHUA S. MARKOWITZ, ESQUIRE (CARCIONE CATTERMOLE, ET AL., 650.367.6811) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JOHN M ANTON, ESQUIRE (BOXER GERSON, 510.286.2993) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT MARILU ANGELES, AS GUARDIAN AD LITEM. ANTONIO RUIZ, ESQUIRE AND CONCHITA LOZANO-BATISTA, ESQUIRE (WEINBERG, ROGER & ROSENFELD, (510.337.1001) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING. The demurrer is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Defendants discharged their duty to plaintiff under Ruttenberg v. Ruttenberg (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 801 by naming and serving plaintiff in the wrongful death action as disclosed by the complaint. Since all of plaintiff's claims turn on this alleged duty, the demurrer is sustained as to the entire complaint. In determining whether plaintiff could amend, the Court has taken judicial notice of certain pleadings in the underlying case - D, E, F and N. Those records confirm plaintiff's service of process, plaintiff's filing of a cross-complaint and plaintiff's recovery of a portion of the settlement after a contested hearing. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
